source: CIS IBM CLOUD FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V1.1.0
title: Ensure Activity Tracker trails are integrated with LogDNA Logs

input_row.source: CIS IBM CLOUD FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V1.1.0
input_row.title: Ensure Activity Tracker trails are integrated with LogDNA Logs
input_row.description: Check whether Activity Tracker trails are integrated with LogDNA Logs.
input_row.rationale: IBM Cloud™ Activity Tracker with LogDNA is a Cloud service that you can use to monitor the activity of your IBM Cloud account. IBM Log Analysis with LogDNA is a service that you can use to analyze and monitor operational logs. Both services offer the same features to manage audit events and logs. However, Activity Tracker trails are not integrated with LogDNA Logs. • The data that is collected and processed by the IBM Cloud Activity Tracker with LogDNA service is associated with that location and not visible to the other regions by virtue of this association. • The data that is collected and processed by the IBM Log Analysis with LogDNA service is associated with that location and not visible to the other regions by virtue of this association. • Within a service instance, data is isolated per LogDNA instance within a region. The IBM Cloud Activity Tracker with LogDNA service offers soft isolation for data storage. Data is mixed together in the same data stores and segmented by tags that are associated with each of the records to enforce access control policies. When audit data and logs are archived for long-term storage, archives of your operational logs and auditing events can be archived to the same IBM Cloud Object Storage (COS) location. Impact: Failure to meet this control will prevent you from managing long-term data, logs and audit events, from a common location.
input_row.audit: Use the following checklist to ensure Activity Tracker trails are integrated with LogDNA Logs: 1. Ensure that IBM Cloud Activity Tracker with LogDNA instances are provisioned with a service plan that includes the ability to configure archiving to an external storage service. 2. Ensure that IBM Log Analysis with LogDNA instances are provisioned with a service plan that includes the ability to configure archiving to an external storage service. 3. Ensure that you have access to storage, for example, check that you have an instance of the IBM Cloud Object Storage service provisioned in your account. 4. Ensure that you have 1 COS bucket available to archive your data. Check the bucket configuration to verify that it meets your corporate and external market regulations. 5. Ensure that each audit instance has archiving enabled and configured to the bucket identified in step 4. 6. Ensure that each logging instance has archiving enabled and configured to the bucket identified in step 4. To check if audit and logging instances are provisioned and configured in the account, complete the following steps: Note: To complete these steps, your IBM Cloud user ID (IBMid) must have the following roles: • A platform role with viewer role for the IBM Cloud Activity Tracker with LogDNA service and the IBM Log Analysis with LogDNA service for the account • A service role with reader role for the IBM Cloud Activity Tracker with LogDNA service and the IBM Log Analysis with LogDNA service for the account • A platform role with viewer role for the IBM Cloud Object Storage service for the account • Identity and Access Management (IAM) permissions to view access groups in the account Console: 1. Log in to IBM Cloud at https://cloud.ibm.com. 2. Go to the Menu icon. Then, select Observability to access the Observability dashboard. 3. Select Activity Tracker from the page navigation menu to see the auditing instances. Select Logging from the page navigation menu to see the logging instances. 4. Check that you can see an Activity Tracker instance in Frankfurt and one instance for each location where you operate in the IBM Cloud. 5. To launch the LogDNA web UI, for each instance, select View LogDNA . 6. In the LogDNA web UI, verify that you can see audit events. 7. Identify the LogDNA instance ID in the LogDNA URL. This ID is used as part of the name of the archive file. CLI instructions: 1. Log in to IBM Cloud. Run the following command: ibmcloud login -a cloud.ibm.com For a federated account, run the following command: ibmcloud login -a cloud.ibm.com --sso 2. List all the audit instances that are provisioned in your account. Run the following command: ibmcloud resource service-instances --service-name logdnaat --all- resource-groups List all the logging instances that are provisioned in your account. Run the following command: ibmcloud resource service-instances --service-name logdna --all- resource-groups 3. For Activity Tracker, check that you have one instance in Frankfurt, and one instance for each location where you operate in the IBM Cloud. 4. Get the LogDNA instance ID. This value is used as part of the archive file name. Run the following command: ibmcloud resource service-instance INSTANCE_NAME --output JSON | grep dashboard_url To check the COS instance and bucket in the account where logs and events are archived, complete the following steps: 1. Identify the CRN of the COS instance where archived data must be located. Run the following command to get the list of instances that are available in the account and their CRNs: ibmcloud resource service-instances --type service_instance --service- name cloud-object-storage --all-resource-groups --output JSON 2. Identify the bucket. Run the following command to list all the buckets that are available in the COS instance: ic cos list-buckets --ibm-service-instance-id <INSTANCE-CRN> 3. For each LogDNA instance, identify the corresponding archive files for that instance. Run the following command: ic cos list-objects --bucket <BUCKET-NAME> --region <REGION-NAME> Note: You must see archive files that include the LogDNA instance ID. If you cannot see any files for that instance, then that instance is not being archived to the bucket. To verify that archiving is enabled in a LogDNA instance, complete the following steps: Note: To complete these steps, your IBM Cloud user ID (IBMid) needs the following roles in addition to the ones specified earlier: • A platform role with operator role for the IBM Cloud Object Storage service for the account • A service role with manager role for the IBM Cloud Activity Tracker with LogDNA service and the IBM Log Analysis with LogDNA service for the account, so that you can verify that archiving is configured  Console: 1. Log in to IBM Cloud at https://cloud.ibm.com. 2. Go to the Menu icon. Then, select Observability to access the Observability dashboard. 3. Select Activity Tracker or Logging from the page navigation menu 4. For each instance, check that archiving is configured for each audit instance. o To launch the LogDNA web UI, select View LogDNA . o In the LogDNA web UI, select Settings . Then, select Archiving . o Verify that archiving is configured for the audit instance. o Check the bucket name. o Check the endpoint value to make sure that the private endpoints are configured. To verify the correct value, you must check the bucket configuration in IBM Cloud Object Storage. o Check the permissions granted to the API key, limit write access to the audit instance only. To verify the permissions, you must identify the credential (service ID) on which you restricted permissions. o Check that the Cloud Object Storage instance ID is the one where data should be archived. You might have different COS instances to archive data.
input_row.remediation: 1. If the option to configure archiving is not allowed, upgrade the instance’s service plan. 2. If archiving is not enabled for an instance, review and define the archiving strategy. Then, configure archiving for an audit instance. 3. If you cannot find an instance of IBM Cloud Object Storage (COS) in your account, provision one. 4. If you want to create a bucket, see create and configure a bucket. Default Value: By default, archiving of events and logs is not enabled for any LogDNA instance.
input_row.impact: Failure to meet this control will prevent you from managing long-term data, logs and audit events, from a common location.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**

After reviewing the provided control, it is evident that the specific audit requirements involve checking the provisioning and configuration of instances within IBM Cloud services, specifically the Activity Tracker with LogDNA and Log Analysis with LogDNA, alongside IBM Cloud Object Storage (COS). The audit process includes using IBM Cloud CLI commands to verify instance provisioning and configuration, making this control suitable for an automated audit approach.

The checklist items in the audit field can be efficiently automated using IBM Cloud APIs and IBM Cloud CLI. These tools allow for the enumeration of resources (service instances, COS buckets), inspection of configurations (archiving settings), and validation of compliance with specified security controls (interaction between LogDNA instances and COS).

**PROGRAM NAME:** ibm_activity_tracker_logdna_integration_verified

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**

1. **API/CLI Tools:**
   - Use `ibmcloud resource service-instances` to list all Activity Tracker and Log Analysis instances.
   - Use `ibmcloud resource service-instance <INSTANCE_NAME>` to retrieve configuration details, including the dashboard URL.
   - Verify archiving settings in the LogDNA web UI by accessing the URL.
   - Use `ibmcloud resource service-instances --type service_instance --service-name cloud-object-storage` to list COS instances.
   - Use `ic cos list-buckets` and `ic cos list-objects` to verify bucket existence and archiving entries.

2. **Checks to Perform:**
   - Ensure each Activity Tracker and Log Analysis instance is provisioned with archiving capabilities.
   - Verify COS instances exist, are accessible, and properly configured for archiving.
   - Confirm archiving is enabled and operational for both audit and logging instances.

3. **Pass/Fail Criteria:**
   - **Pass:** All activity tracker and log analysis instances are integrated with COS, archiving is enabled, and archive entries are present in the designated buckets.
   - **Fail:** Any missing provisioning, incorrect configurations, or absence of archived data in COS for the specified instances.

This automated approach ensures a consistent and thorough assessment of the configuration and integration between Activity Tracker trails and LogDNA logs with IBM Cloud Object Storage for compliance verification.
step2_review:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE

- The classification of the audit approach as Automated is appropriate. The tasks involved, such as listing service instances and verifying configurations, can be efficiently performed using IBM Cloud APIs and IBM Cloud CLI commands. The use of these tools aligns with the capabilities of IBM Cloud SDK and CLI, making automation feasible and effective for this control.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 8

- The automation details provided are largely correct, with valid IBM Cloud CLI commands for listing service instances and verifying configurations. However, the step involving verification of archiving settings in the LogDNA web UI is not fully automated, as it requires manual inspection. The program name, while descriptive, could be more specific by including terms like 'archiving_enabled' to clarify the specific security check being performed.

**IMPROVEMENTS:**

- **Archiving Verification:** The step to verify archiving settings in the LogDNA web UI should be automated if possible. Consider using IBM Cloud API calls to check the archiving configuration directly, if supported.
  
- **Program Name:** A more descriptive program name could be `ibm_activity_tracker_logdna_archiving_integration_verified` to specify the focus on archiving integration.

- **Alternative Methods:** Explore using IBM Cloud Config to automate compliance checks and ensure configurations meet security standards. This could provide a more comprehensive and automated approach to verifying compliance.

- **Missing Considerations:** Ensure that the audit process includes checks for data integrity and security settings within COS, such as encryption and access controls, to provide a more holistic compliance assessment.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

- The assessment demonstrates a strong understanding of IBM Cloud services and the capabilities of IBM Cloud CLI. The approach is well-structured and aligns with security best practices, providing confidence in its effectiveness.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:**

- **Modify:** Enhance the automation by exploring IBM Cloud API capabilities for verifying archiving settings directly, and update the program name to be more descriptive of the specific security check. Additionally, consider integrating IBM Cloud Config for a more comprehensive compliance check.
